description.description: Reference compounds spanning the rotatable-bond spectrum (0 → 19). Used by flexibility-comparison tests to surface divergence between the fast 3D endpoint (10 conformers) and the Boltzmann async endpoint (50-300 adaptive conformers). Rigid compounds serve as same-math regression gates; flexible compounds show how much more thorough conformer sampling actually shifts ensemble-averaged descriptors.

columns.name: Short identifier
columns.flexibility_class: One of: rigid, semi_rigid, flexible, very_flexible
columns.notes: Why this compound is in the set

compounds.name: benzene
compounds.notes: Single aromatic ring, 0 rot bonds — all conformers identical

compounds.name: aspirin
compounds.notes: Aromatic core with 2 rot bonds in the acetate + COOH

compounds.name: hexane
compounds.notes: Small alkane, 3 rot bonds — modest conformational spread

compounds.name: ibuprofen_s
compounds.notes: Drug with isobutyl + carboxylic-acid chains, 4 rot bonds

compounds.name: decane
compounds.notes: C10 alkane, 7 rot bonds — extended vs compact conformers differ

compounds.name: dodecane
compounds.notes: C12 alkane, 9 rot bonds — Boltzmann tier 200 conformers

compounds.name: hexadecane
compounds.notes: C16 alkane, 13 rot bonds — Boltzmann tier 300 conformers

compounds.name: docosane
compounds.notes: C22 alkane, 19 rot bonds — max conformational spread in this set
